description: Mobile team Slack standup rollup. Tests robustness: the transcript mixes real product asks with off-topic chatter (lunch, music, vacation). Should produce stories about search ranking + Apple Pay POS, ignore the chatter. Expect a duplicate against NS-251 (search purchase-history ranking) and NS-243 (Apple Pay / Google Pay at POS).

deterministic_metrics.name: acceptance_criteria_well_formed
observations: 5/5 stories have AC count in range

deterministic_metrics.name: expected_duplicates_found
observations: Expected duplicate (['search', 'ranking', 'purchase history'] ↔ NS-251) not found at confidence ≥ high
Expected duplicate (['apple pay', 'google pay', 'pos'] ↔ NS-243) not found at confidence ≥ medium

reasons.ac_quality: Most criteria are concrete and testable with clear Given/When/Then structure, though ST-01's '2 percentage point decrease' criterion is measurable but may be difficult to attribute causally within the two-week window.
reasons.priority_justification: ST-01 and ST-02 provide solid quantitative justification (4% abandonment increase, 12% relevance improvement), but ST-03/ST-04 rationales are somewhat circular ('parity feature... should be implemented simultaneously') and ST-05's 'UI polish' reasoning is vague about impact.
reasons.story_granularity: ST-01 is appropriately sized as a single mobile feature, but ST-03 and ST-04 are nearly identical POS stories that should likely be combined (they share firmware partner dependency and differ only by wallet provider), creating artificial decomposition.
reasons.tag_accuracy: Tags are generally appropriate and consistent (mobile-app, pos, payments, loyalty), though the guardrail findings correctly note non-canonical tags like 'checkout', 'hardware', 'ui' that should be standardized.
reasons.conflict_reasoning: No conflicts were flagged and correctly so—all stories explicitly reference the PaymentGateway constraint (C-05) in their descriptions and none violate the stated architectural rules.

llm_judge.overall_comment: This is a well-structured synthesis with strong traceability from topics to stories and mostly testable acceptance criteria. The main weaknesses are splitting Apple Pay and Google Pay POS work into separate stories (ST-03/ST-04) when they share identical infrastructure dependencies, and some hand-wavy priority justifications for lower-priority items that lean on generic benefits rather than quantified business impact.
